src/flit/widgets/directionality

      • widgetTypeName(w: Directionality): string

Directionality: an InheritedWidget that propagates a text direction (left-to-right or right-to-left) down the widget tree. Widgets read it via textDirectionOf(ctx); layout widgets that care (Row, Text, Align with start/end alignments) honor it.

flit's existing layout widgets are all written in LTR-first terms. Directionality lets you flip a subtree to RTL without changing any widget code, and is the bedrock of any internationalized app.

Imports

../foundation/widget, ../foundation/key, ../foundation/geometry

Types

Directionality = ref object of InheritedWidget
  direction*: TextDirection
Provides a TextDirection to its subtree. Read via textDirectionOf(ctx); defaults to tdLtr when no ancestor is found.

Procs

proc directionality(direction: TextDirection; child: Widget; key: Key = nil): Directionality {.
    ...raises: [], tags: [], forbids: [].}

Wraps child in a directionality scope.

Inputs:

  • direction: tdLtr or tdRtl.
  • child: the subtree that should observe this direction.
  • key: reconciliation key.
proc textDirectionOf(ctx: BuildContext): TextDirection {....raises: [], tags: [],
    forbids: [].}
Returns the nearest enclosing text direction, or tdLtr if none is set. Subscribes the calling element to the Directionality so it rebuilds when the direction changes.

    forbids: [].}
